content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Resolved all 5 pending amendments from round-2 review for the success reconciliation handler (bead 9ni.8.5.1). Amendment 1: Moved try_reconcile_success before mark_completed at both call sites (multi-repo and single-repo), so a daemon crash during reconciliation causes task reprocessing rather than permanently skipping bead close/sync/milestone update. Amendment 2: Added task_id conflict check and merge to merge_completion_journal_details in service.rs, preventing silent drop during journal event deduplication. Amendment 3: Changed mark_milestone_failed closure and reconcile_success call to use project_dir instead of base_dir for all milestone operations; removed unused base_dir parameter from try_reconcile_success, leaving only project_dir (single-repo passes base_dir, multi-repo passes repo_root). Amendment 4: BrMutationAdapter, BrAdapter, and BvAdapter now constructed with with_working_dir(project_dir) so br show/close/sync and bv --robot-next execute in the correct repository directory. Amendment 5: render_completion_journal_details now accepts task_id parameter (all existing callers pass None); completion_journal_details() passes self.task_id.as_deref(); finalize_task_run_internal, backfill_terminal_entry, and replace_terminal_entry extract structured task_id from outcome_detail 'task_id=<value>' format and set entry.task_id. End-to-end test updated to assert structural task_id field on lineage entry.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Moved try_reconcile_success before DaemonTaskService::mark_completed at both call sites in daemon_loop.rs (multi-repo process_task_multi_repo and single-repo process_task), ensuring a crash during reconciliation causes task reprocessing rather than permanently skipping bead close/sync/milestone update
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: Added task_id conflict check (option_conflicts) and merge (if merged.task_id.is_none()) to merge_completion_journal_details in service.rs, preventing silent drop during journal event deduplication
3. [Completed] Amendment 3: Changed mark_milestone_failed closure to use project_dir for update_status call; changed reconcile_success to receive project_dir for milestone mutations; removed unused base_dir parameter from try_reconcile_success signature (single-repo passes base_dir as project_dir, multi-repo passes repo_root)
4. [Completed] Amendment 4: BrAdapter and BvAdapter now constructed with with_working_dir(project_dir.to_path_buf()) so br show/close/sync and bv --robot-next run in the repository that owns the .beads/ graph rather than the daemon process cwd
5. [Completed] Amendment 5: render_completion_journal_details accepts task_id: Option<&str> parameter (all ~15 existing callers pass None); completion_journal_details() passes self.task_id.as_deref(); finalize_task_run_internal, backfill_terminal_entry, and replace_terminal_entry extract task_id from outcome_detail 'task_id=<value>' format and set entry.task_id structurally; end-to-end test updated to assert entry.task_id == Some('task-e2e-123')
6. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt (applied to daemon_loop.rs and service.rs); cargo fmt --check (clean); cargo clippy (16 pre-existing warnings, 0 new); cargo test (757 passed, 0 failed)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 757 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (full suite)
- cargo test automation_runtime::success_reconciliation: 9 passed (8 unit + 1 end-to-end), 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 16 pre-existing warnings, 0 new
- cargo check: compiles cleanly
- End-to-end test reconcile_success_end_to_end_updates_milestone passes with structural task_id assertion: entry.task_id == Some('task-e2e-123')
- Amendment 1 verified: try_reconcile_success precedes mark_completed at both call sites
- Amendment 2 verified: merge_completion_journal_details includes task_id conflict check and merge logic
- Amendment 3 verified: try_reconcile_success reduced to (project_dir, task); mark_milestone_failed uses project_dir; reconcile_success receives project_dir
- Amendment 4 verified: BrAdapter and BvAdapter constructed with with_working_dir(project_dir.to_path_buf())
- Amendment 5 verified: render_completion_journal_details accepts task_id param; completion_journal_details() passes self.task_id; fs.rs extraction populates entry.task_id from outcome_detail in finalize/backfill/replace paths

## Outstanding Risks

- The lineage task_id extraction parses the 'task_id=<value>' format from outcome_detail in finalize_task_run_internal, backfill_terminal_entry, and replace_terminal_entry. This couples the fs adapter to a format defined in success_reconciliation.rs. A future refactor should thread task_id as a first-class parameter through the record_bead_completion/update_task_run chain to eliminate this format dependency.
- Moving reconciliation before mark_completed means a crash during reconciliation will cause the task to be reprocessed on restart. The reconciliation is idempotent (br close checks status, sync is unconditional, record_bead_completion handles replay), so reprocessing is safe but will produce duplicate log output.
- The bv --robot-next hint is still captured in-memory only and not persisted to disk — downstream consumers (selection step in 9ni.8.6) will need the ReconciliationOutcome returned by reconcile_success to act on the hint
